name: Google Gemini API
what_it_does: Google's multimodal LLM API with 1M+ token context, grounding with Google Search, and native tool calling for AI agents.
use_cases: Processing and analyzing very long documents (1M+ token context window)
Multimodal agents that reason over images, video, audio, and text together
Code generation and analysis with Gemini Code models
Grounded generation that cites real-time Google Search results
Function calling agents with structured JSON output
not_for: Applications requiring on-premise or self-hosted LLM deployment
Use cases where Google data usage policies are a concern
Teams needing SLA-backed enterprise contracts without Google Cloud
best_when: You need extremely long context windows, real-time grounding with Google Search, or multimodal reasoning across diverse media types.
avoid_when: You need predictable latency under 200ms or are in regulated environments where Google data processing is prohibited.

auth.notes: API key for AI Studio (dev), service account/Application Default Credentials for GCP Vertex AI production. Two separate surfaces: AI Studio (generativelanguage.googleapis.com) vs Vertex AI (different endpoint, billing).

pricing.free_tier_limits: Gemini 1.5 Flash: 15 RPM, 1M tokens/min, 1500 requests/day free via AI Studio
pricing.paid_tiers: Gemini 2.0 Flash: $0.075/1M input tokens, $0.30/1M output tokens; Flash Thinking: higher rates; Pro models: higher still
pricing.notes: Free tier via AI Studio API key. Vertex AI requires GCP billing. Context caching available at 75% discount for cached tokens.

performance.rate_limits: Varies by model and tier; Flash: 15 RPM free, 2000 RPM paid; Pro: lower limits

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: Standard Google API error format with status codes and messages; quota exceeded messages reference specific quota types
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: No request idempotency keys; each call is independent
known_agent_gotchas: Two separate APIs: AI Studio (generativelanguage.googleapis.com) and Vertex AI have different auth, endpoints, and feature parity
Function calling response requires extracting from candidates[0].content.parts[0].functionCall — deeply nested
Grounding with Google Search adds latency and may not be available in all regions
Context caching requires explicit cache creation step; not automatic like Anthropic's prompt caching
Safety filters can silently truncate or refuse responses; check finish_reason=SAFETY in response

security_score_components.security_notes: API keys scoped to project; service accounts support fine-grained IAM on Vertex. Data sent to Google for processing per their privacy policy.
